title: Issue Breakdown: Decompose Into Epics + Child Issues
description:
Using the Spec (mcq-7h6) as input, decompose the refactor into bd epics and child issues. Each child issue must be small, independently implementable, and have acceptance criteria.

Suggested epic areas:
- Data Layer: Extract scenario data management, define data contracts
- UI Components: Refactor/split components, establish patterns
- State Management: Replace monolithic GameContext, introduce proper state architecture
- Testing Infrastructure: Unit tests, integration tests, E2E setup
- Accessibility: a11y audit, ARIA, keyboard nav, screen reader support
- Build Tooling: TypeScript config, linting rules, CI pipeline

Each child issue should:
- Be completable in a single session (< 4 hours of work)
- Have clear acceptance criteria
- Specify dependencies on other issues where ordering matters
- Include a time estimate
acceptance_criteria:
1. Epics created in bd (type epic) for each major area identified in the Spec
2. Each epic has child task issues (created with --parent)
3. Every child issue has acceptance criteria and a time estimate
4. Dependency links set between issues where ordering matters
5. bd graph produces a readable dependency graph of the full refactor
6. No issue is larger than ~4 hours of estimated work

title: Onboarding and First-Run Experience
description: Fix onboarding state sync so FirstTradeGuide appears after completion, and change the default scenario from ESPN Live to Midweek with a proper empty-state fallback.

title: Sync Onboarding completion with OnboardingProvider state
description: When onboarding completes, the OnboardingProvider state does not update because Onboarding.tsx writes to localStorage directly instead of calling markOnboardingComplete. The FirstTradeGuide will not appear until page refresh. Fix Onboarding.tsx to call the provider method. Audit issue: H-1.
design: Modify src/components/Onboarding/Onboarding.tsx. Import useOnboarding from OnboardingProvider. In handleComplete, call markOnboardingComplete() from the context instead of writing localStorage directly. The provider markOnboardingComplete already handles localStorage + state updates. Remove redundant localStorage.setItem calls and CustomEvent dispatch if unnecessary.
acceptance_criteria:
1. After completing onboarding, hasCompletedOnboarding updates to true without page refresh
2. FirstTradeGuide appears on the Market page immediately after onboarding completion
3. showFirstTradeGuide state activates within the same session
4. Existing onboarding tests pass

title: Change default scenario from ESPN Live to Midweek and add ESPN empty-state fallback
description: ESPN Live as the default scenario shows an empty first impression with zero counters and no events. Change default to Midweek so new users see populated data. Add an empty-state fallback for ESPN Live with a clear message. Audit issue: H-8.
design: Modify src/context/ScenarioContext.tsx to change default scenario from espn-live to midweek. Add an empty-state component in src/pages/Timeline/Timeline.tsx that detects espn-live with zero events and shows a helpful message. Check src/context/SimulationContext.tsx for espnError propagation.
acceptance_criteria:
1. App loads with Midweek scenario selected by default
2. When ESPN Live is selected and has no data, a clear empty state message appears
3. ESPN refresh button shows loading state and on failure displays an error message
4. User can still manually select ESPN Live from the scenario toggle

title: UI Components: Restructure, Error Boundaries, and Code Splitting
description: Restructure components into co-located directories, move presentational components to shared/, add ErrorBoundary, implement route-based code splitting, migrate CSS to CSS Modules, and convert all components/pages to TypeScript.

title: Create ErrorBoundary component
description: Create a shared ErrorBoundary component that catches rendering errors per-page without crashing the entire app. Displays a user-friendly error fallback with option to retry.
acceptance_criteria:
1. shared/ErrorBoundary/ErrorBoundary.tsx exists as a class component (React error boundaries require class components)
2. Catches errors in child component tree and renders a fallback UI
3. Fallback shows error message and a 'Try Again' button that resets the boundary
4. Accepts a custom fallback prop for page-specific error UIs
5. Logs errors to console for debugging

title: Move shared components to src/shared/
description: Move context-free presentational components from components/ to shared/ in co-located directory structure. Components: EventMarkerPopup, InfoTooltip, SkeletonLoader, FirstTradeGuide, Glossary. Update all import paths.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Each shared component in its own directory: shared/<Name>/<Name>.jsx + <Name>.css
2. Zero imports from context/, services/, or data/ in any shared component
3. All import paths updated across the codebase
4. Barrel file shared/index.js re-exports all shared components
5. Build succeeds with no broken imports
6. Cypress E2E tests pass

title: Restructure feature components into co-located directories
description: Move each feature component from flat files (components/Foo.jsx + components/Foo.css) into co-located directories (components/Foo/Foo.jsx + Foo.css). Split Toast and Onboarding providers from their UI components. Update barrel exports and all import paths.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Each feature component in its own directory: components/<Name>/<Name>.jsx + <Name>.css
2. Toast split into Toast/Toast.jsx (UI) and Toast/ToastProvider.jsx (context)
3. Onboarding split into Onboarding/Onboarding.jsx (UI) and Onboarding/OnboardingProvider.jsx (context)
4. components/index.js barrel updated
5. All import paths updated across the codebase
6. Build succeeds, Cypress E2E tests pass

title: Restructure pages into co-located directories
description: Move each page from flat files (pages/Foo.jsx + pages/Foo.css) into co-located directories (pages/Foo/Foo.jsx + Foo.css). Update all import paths in App.jsx routing.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Each page in its own directory: pages/<Name>/<Name>.jsx + <Name>.css
2. App.jsx route imports updated to new paths
3. Build succeeds, Cypress E2E tests pass

title: Add route-based code splitting with React.lazy and Suspense
description: Wrap all page imports in App.tsx with React.lazy() for route-based code splitting. Add Suspense boundaries with SkeletonLoader fallbacks. Wrap each route with ErrorBoundary. Each page becomes a separate Vite chunk loaded on demand.
acceptance_criteria:
1. All page imports in App.tsx use React.lazy(() => import(...))
2. Each Route element wrapped with ErrorBoundary and Suspense
3. Suspense fallback uses SkeletonLoader
4. vite build produces separate chunks per page
5. Pages load correctly on navigation (no flash of blank screen)
6. Initial bundle size further reduced (verify with vite build output)
7. Cypress E2E tests pass

title: Fix Leaderboard to use real league data
description: Replace hardcoded fake trader data in Leaderboard.jsx with getLeaderboardRankings() from SocialContext (or GameContext during transition). The function already exists and uses real league member data — the page just doesn't call it (M2).
acceptance_criteria:
1. Leaderboard page uses getLeaderboardRankings() from context — no hardcoded fake data
2. Rankings reflect actual portfolio values and league member holdings
3. User's row correctly positioned in rankings
4. Medal icons for top 3 preserved
5. Cypress leaderboard tests pass

title: Migrate CSS to CSS Modules
description: Convert all 24 component/page CSS files from global CSS to co-located CSS Modules. Each component gets a .module.css file with scoped class names. Replace className strings with styles.foo imports. Keep index.css for global reset and CSS custom properties only.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Every component/page uses a co-located .module.css file
2. No global component/page CSS files remain (only index.css for reset + tokens)
3. className attributes use styles.foo pattern (import styles from './<Name>.module.css')
4. No visual regressions — app looks identical before and after
5. index.css contains only global reset, CSS custom properties, and font imports
6. Build succeeds, Cypress E2E tests pass

title: Convert components and pages to TypeScript
description: Convert all shared components, feature components, pages, App.jsx, and main.jsx from .jsx to .tsx. Add TypeScript props interfaces for every component. This is the final TypeScript migration phase — by this point, all contexts, services, and types are already in TypeScript.
acceptance_criteria:
1. All .jsx files converted to .tsx (zero .jsx files remain in src/)
2. Every component has a TypeScript props interface (no implicit any props)
3. App.tsx and main.tsx converted as the final step
4. No any types in component files
5. Build succeeds with strict: true, no type errors
6. Cypress E2E tests pass

title: Trading UX Improvements
description: Fix silent portfolio reset on scenario switch, add explanation to disabled Sell tab, and fix PlayerCard moveReason truncation.

title: Add confirmation dialog before portfolio reset on scenario switch
description: When users switch scenarios, their portfolio is silently reset. Add a confirmation dialog warning about data loss. The dialog should appear only when the user has a non-empty portfolio. Audit issue: H-2.
design: Modify src/components/ScenarioToggle/ScenarioToggle.tsx. Import useTrading and check if portfolio is non-empty before calling setScenario. If non-empty, show window.confirm() dialog (MVP) or a styled modal. Modify both onClick handler and handleMobileSelect function.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Switching scenarios when portfolio is non-empty shows a confirmation dialog
2. Dialog warns about portfolio and cash reset
3. Dialog has Switch & Reset and Cancel buttons
4. Cancel keeps current scenario and portfolio intact
5. Switch & Reset proceeds with scenario change
6. Empty portfolio does not trigger dialog

title: Add tooltip explanation to disabled Sell tab
description: The Sell tab on PlayerDetail is disabled when !holding but has no explanation. Add a tooltip or title attribute that says You need to own shares to sell. Audit issue: H-3.
design: Modify src/pages/PlayerDetail/PlayerDetail.tsx around line 681-692. Add title="You need to own shares to sell" to the disabled button element. For a richer experience, wrap in a tooltip container using the Tooltip component from Onboarding.tsx or a simple title attribute.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Hovering over disabled Sell tab shows You need to own shares to sell
2. When Sell tab is enabled (user holds shares), no tooltip appears
3. Tooltip disappears when mouse leaves the tab

title: Fix PlayerCard moveReason truncation to respect word boundaries
description: PlayerCard.tsx line 140 uses substring(0, 60) which cuts mid-word, and unconditionally appends ellipsis. Fix to truncate at word boundaries and only add ellipsis when actually truncated. Audit issue: H-5.
design: Modify src/components/PlayerCard/PlayerCard.tsx line 140. Replace player.moveReason.substring(0, 60)}... with a helper function truncateAtWord(text, 60) that finds last space before position 60, slices there, appends ellipsis. If full string is <=60 chars, return unchanged.
acceptance_criteria:
1. moveReason truncation respects word boundaries
2. Ellipsis is only appended when text was actually truncated (length > limit)
3. Short move reasons display without trailing ellipsis

title: Add Vitest tests for Watchlist page (0% coverage)
description: - type: task

title: CSS Module Class Fixes
description: Fix CSS module class references that use raw strings instead of styles[...] references. These are Critical severity because they break styling across onboarding, navigation, player detail content tiles, and daily mission result chips.

title: Fix Onboarding content wrapper CSS module class
description: The onboarding content wrapper div on line 146 of Onboarding.tsx uses a raw string onboarding-content instead of styles['onboarding-content']. The highlight class highlight-${currentStep.highlight} is also raw. Fix both to use CSS module references so that padding, text-align, and highlight styles apply correctly. Audit issue: C-1.
design: Modify src/components/Onboarding/Onboarding.tsx line 146. Change className={`onboarding-content ${...}`} to className={`${styles['onboarding-content']} ${currentStep.highlight ? styles[`highlight-${currentStep.highlight}`] || \\ : \\\}`}. If highlight classes (highlight-virtual, highlight-colors, highlight-cta) do not exist in Onboarding.module.css, add them or remove the conditional class. Verify the onboarding-content padding and text-align are applied.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Content wrapper div uses styles['onboarding-content'] instead of raw onboarding-content
2. Highlight classes use CSS module references or are removed if unused in the CSS
3. Onboarding content area displays with padding: 20px 40px 40px and text-align: center
4. Existing tests pass

title: Fix Layout nav link CSS module classes
description: All six NavLink components in Layout.tsx use raw nav-link and active strings. Since the component uses CSS Modules, these must be changed to styles['nav-link'] and styles['active'] respectively. Navigation is completely unstyled without this fix. Audit issue: C-2.
design: Modify src/components/Layout/Layout.tsx lines 78, 89, 99, 109, 119, 129. Change className={({ isActive }) => `nav-link ${isActive ? 'active' : ''}`} to className={({ isActive }) => `${styles['nav-link']} ${isActive ? styles['active'] : ''}`}. The CSS module already defines .nav-link and .nav-link.active styles.
acceptance_criteria:
1. All six NavLink components use styles['nav-link'] for the base class
2. Active state uses styles['active'] via the isActive callback
3. Navigation links display with correct padding, font-weight, and active state highlighting
4. Existing tests pass

title: Fix PlayerDetail content tile type badge CSS module classes
description: Content tile type badges in PlayerDetail.tsx line 483 use ${tile.type} as a raw class name alongside the module-referenced styles['tile-type']. Fix to use CSS module references for the type classes so color-coded backgrounds apply. Audit issue: M-2.
design: Modify src/pages/PlayerDetail/PlayerDetail.tsx line 483. Change className={`${styles['tile-type']} ${tile.type}`} to className={`${styles['tile-type']} ${styles[tile.type] || ''}`}. Verify that PlayerDetail.module.css defines classes for each tile type (.article, .video, .analysis, .news).
acceptance_criteria:
1. Content tile type badges use styles[tile.type] or equivalent CSS module reference
2. Different content types (article, video, analysis, news) show distinct color-coded backgrounds
3. Existing tests pass

title: Fix DailyMission result-chip status CSS module classes
description: Result chips in DailyMission.tsx (lines 111 and 135) append ${status} as a raw class where status is correct or incorrect. Fix to use styles[status] for proper CSS module references so green/red feedback renders. Audit issue: M-3.
design: Modify src/components/DailyMission/DailyMission.tsx lines 111 and 135. Change className={`${styles['result-chip']} ${status}`} to className={`${styles['result-chip']} ${status ? styles[status] : ''}`}. Verify that DailyMission.module.css defines .correct and .incorrect classes.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Result chips use styles[status] instead of raw strings
2. Correct predictions display with green styling, incorrect with red
3. Existing tests pass

title: Test issue for workflow validation
Spec: Write Refactor Specification
description:
Synthesize Research and Tech Stack findings into a refactor specification. Define the target architecture, modularization plan, and acceptance criteria per major area.

This is the central planning document that Issue Breakdown will use to generate epics and child issues.

Areas to cover:
- Target state management approach (replace monolithic GameContext with what?)
- Service layer boundaries and API contracts
- Component hierarchy and reuse strategy
- Directory structure / module boundaries
- Migration strategy (incremental vs. big-bang, feature-flag approach)
- Acceptance criteria for each major area (data layer, UI components, state mgmt, testing, a11y, build tooling)
acceptance_criteria:
1. Target architecture defined (state management approach, service layer boundaries, component hierarchy)
2. Modularization plan documented (what moves where, new directory structure)
3. Acceptance criteria written for each major area (data layer, UI components, state management, testing, a11y, build tooling)
4. Migration strategy outlined (incremental vs. big-bang, feature-flag approach)
5. Spec reviewed and approved before Issue Breakdown begins

title: Add Vitest tests for Mission page (0% coverage)

Requires shared test helpers, new unit tests for 8 uncovered files, expanded partial coverage, data-testid attributes on ~20 components, and Cypress selector migration from broken CSS class selectors to data-testid.

title: Create shared test helpers (renderWithProviders + mockData)
description: Create src/test/renderWithProviders.tsx (wraps components in mocked ScenarioContext, TradingContext, SocialContext, ToastContext) and src/test/mockData.ts (reusable mock player, portfolio, leaderboard data). Base on existing mock patterns in Leaderboard.test.tsx and PlayerCard.test.tsx. Refactor one existing test to validate the helper works.
acceptance_criteria:
1. src/test/renderWithProviders.tsx exists and exports renderWithProviders()
2. src/test/mockData.ts exports mockPlayer, mockPlayers, mockPortfolio, mockLeaderboardRankings
3. All 617 existing Vitest tests still pass via npm run test:run

title: Fix all Cypress E2E tests -- migrate selectors from CSS classes to data-testid
description: Update all 10 Cypress spec files + cypress/support/commands.js to use [data-testid="..."] selectors instead of broken .css-class selectors. Pattern: cy.get('.player-card') becomes cy.get('[data-testid="player-card"]'). Files: market.cy.js, player-detail.cy.js, timeline.cy.js, watchlist.cy.js, portfolio.cy.js, mission.cy.js, onboarding.cy.js, toasts.cy.js, leaderboard.cy.js, navigation.cy.js, commands.js.
acceptance_criteria:
1. All 10 Cypress spec files updated with data-testid selectors
2. commands.js getPlayerCards() uses [data-testid="player-card"]
3. At least 70 of 80 Cypress tests pass when run via npm run cy:run
4. Cypress line coverage reaches 75%+ via npm run cy:coverage

title: Verify coverage targets met
description: Run npm run test:coverage and npm run cy:coverage. Verify Vitest line coverage >= 80% and Cypress line coverage >= 75%. Generate final HTML reports.
acceptance_criteria:
1. npm run test:coverage shows >= 80% line coverage
2. npm run cy:coverage shows >= 75% line coverage
3. coverage/vitest/index.html and coverage/cypress/lcov-report/index.html both generated

title: Add Vitest tests for Market page (0% coverage)
description: Write src/pages/Market/__tests__/Market.test.tsx. Test player grid rendering, 4 sort tabs (risers/fallers/active/price), search filtering (partial match, case-insensitive, no results), welcome banner dismiss with localStorage persistence, loading skeleton state. Mock useScenario, useTrading, PlayerCard, MiniLeaderboard, FirstTradeGuide, framer-motion.

1. Market.test.tsx exists with tests for: grid rendering, all 4 sort modes, search filtering, welcome banner dismiss, empty state
2. Market.tsx line coverage reaches 80%+ per npm run test:coverage
3. All tests pass via npm run test:run

title: Add Vitest tests for Watchlist page (0% coverage)
description: Write src/pages/Watchlist/__tests__/Watchlist.test.tsx. Test empty state with illustration, quick-add popular players, remove from watchlist with toast, watchlist grid rendering, Browse All Players navigation link. Mock useTrading, useSocial, useToast, PlayerCard, framer-motion.
acceptance_criteria:

2. Watchlist.tsx line coverage reaches 80%+ per npm run test:coverage

title: Add Vitest tests for Mission page (0% coverage)
description: Write src/pages/Mission/__tests__/Mission.test.tsx. Test DailyMission renders, help panel toggle with localStorage persistence, collapsible sections. Mock DailyMission component, framer-motion.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Mission.test.tsx exists with tests for: page rendering, help toggle, localStorage persistence
2. Mission.tsx line coverage reaches 80%+ per npm run test:coverage

description: Write src/components/Onboarding/__tests__/Onboarding.test.tsx. Test 6-step wizard navigation (next/back), skip button closes modal, Escape key closes modal, localStorage persistence for completed/dismissed state, step indicators update, custom event dispatch (mcqueen-onboarding-complete) on completion.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Onboarding.test.tsx exists with tests for: step navigation, skip, Escape key, localStorage, step indicators
2. Onboarding.tsx line coverage reaches 80%+ per npm run test:coverage

description: Write src/components/DailyMission/__tests__/DailyMission.test.tsx. Test pick selection for risers/fallers columns, reveal results, reset mission, score calculation and display, disabled states when picks incomplete, collapsible mode. Mock useTrading and useSocial contexts.

description: Write tests for three small 0%-coverage components. PlayoffAnnouncementModal: show/dismiss with localStorage persistence. MiniLeaderboard: top-3 medals, user position, gap calculation, empty state. LiveTicker: conditional render for live scenario only, event display, fallback to history.

PlayerDetail (47% to 75%): add tests for buy/sell flows, quantity validation, watchlist toggle, error state for invalid player. ScenarioToggle (48% to 80%): switching and active state. Glossary (48% to 80%): open/close, search. SkeletonLoader (11% to 80%): all variants. InfoTooltip (45% to 80%): hover behavior.

Components: Market.tsx, PlayerDetail.tsx, Timeline.tsx, Watchlist.tsx, Portfolio.tsx, Mission.tsx, DailyMission.tsx, Onboarding.tsx, PlayerCard.tsx, Toast.tsx/ToastProvider.tsx, Leaderboard.tsx. Each data-testid matches the original class name (e.g. data-testid="player-card").

2. All Vitest tests still pass (no regressions)
3. npm run build succeeds

title: Add Vitest tests for Onboarding component (0% coverage)
description: - type: task

title: Spec: UI Audit Repair Specification
description:
Ingest the UI audit research (.ralph/research/ui-audit.md) and produce a detailed specification for refactoring and UI repair. The audit identified 30 issues (2 Critical, 8 High, 11 Medium, 9 Low) spanning CSS module class bugs, layout problems, UX friction, data display issues, and accessibility gaps.

Follow the spec template in .ralph/SPEC_PROMPT.md with SPEC_NAME=ui-repair. Explore the current codebase to verify each finding against actual source files. Produce:
1. .ralph/specs/ui-repair.md -- full specification with epics, beads, acceptance criteria, and design notes
2. .ralph/specs/ui-repair-beads.sh -- runnable bd create script to populate the tracker

Group findings into ~8 epics: CSS Module Fixes, Layout/Header Repair, Onboarding/First-Run, Trading UX, Timeline/Scenario, Mission/Leaderboard, Accessibility, and Minor Polish. Order by dependency so CSS module fixes come first (Critical path) and polish comes last.
design:
Template: .ralph/SPEC_PROMPT.md (SPEC_NAME=ui-repair)
Research input: .ralph/research/ui-audit.md
Conventions: .ralph/AGENT.md
Output dir: .ralph/specs/ (create with mkdir -p)

Epic ordering (suggested):
1. CSS Module Class Fixes (C-1, C-2, M-2, M-3) -- no deps, Critical
2. Layout/Header Repair (M-4, M-10, M-11) -- depends on Epic 1
3. Onboarding/First-Run (H-1, H-8) -- depends on Epic 1
4. Trading UX (H-2, H-3, H-5) -- independent
5. Timeline/Scenario (H-4, H-7, M-9, L-4, L-7, L-8) -- independent
6. Mission/Leaderboard (H-6, M-6, L-6) -- independent
7. Accessibility (M-1, M-7, M-8) -- after functional fixes
8. Minor Polish (M-5, L-1, L-2, L-3, L-5, L-9) -- last

Key source files to explore:
- src/components/Onboarding/Onboarding.tsx + .module.css (C-1, H-1)
- src/components/Layout/Layout.tsx + .module.css (C-2, M-10, M-11)
- src/pages/PlayerDetail/PlayerDetail.tsx (M-2, M-5, H-3, H-5, L-1)
- src/pages/Market/Market.tsx (M-4, L-3)
- src/components/DailyMission/DailyMission.tsx (M-3, H-6)
- src/context/TradingContext.tsx (H-2)
- src/context/OnboardingProvider.tsx (H-1)
- src/pages/Timeline/Timeline.tsx (H-4, L-3)
- src/components/ScenarioToggle/ (M-9, H-8)
- src/components/TimelineDebugger/ (H-7)
- src/components/Toast/ (M-7)
- src/pages/Watchlist/ (M-8)
- src/pages/Leaderboard/ (M-6, L-6)
- src/components/LiveTicker/ (L-4)
- src/components/PlayerCard/ (H-5, M-1)
acceptance_criteria:
1. .ralph/specs/ui-repair.md exists following full SPEC_PROMPT.md structure
2. Every audit issue (C-1 through L-9, 30 total) mapped to a bead or listed in Out of Scope
3. Each bead has: type, priority, estimate, dependencies, description, acceptance criteria, design notes with file paths
4. .ralph/specs/ui-repair-beads.sh is valid runnable shell script creating all epics and child beads
5. Design notes reference verified current file paths (not just audit line numbers)
6. Beads ordered so bd ready surfaces them in correct dependency sequence

title: Build Tooling Foundation
description: Set up TypeScript configuration and Vite build optimizations as the foundation for the entire refactor. All other epics depend on this infrastructure being in place.

title: Add tsconfig.json with strict mode
description: Add tsconfig.json configured with strict: true. Vite handles .ts/.tsx natively so no build config changes needed. Existing .jsx files continue to work alongside new .tsx files during migration.
acceptance_criteria:
1. tsconfig.json exists with strict: true, jsx: react-jsx, and appropriate include/exclude paths
2. vite build succeeds with no new errors
3. A new .tsx file can be created and imported from an existing .jsx file
4. Existing Cypress E2E tests still pass

title: Configure Vite vendor chunking
description: Add manualChunks config to vite.config.js to split recharts, framer-motion, and react-router-dom into separate cacheable vendor chunks. This reduces the main bundle size and improves caching.
acceptance_criteria:
1. vite.config.js has build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks splitting recharts, framer-motion, and react-router-dom
2. vite build produces separate vendor chunk files for each library
3. vite build produces no size warnings
4. App loads and functions correctly in preview mode

title: Add Vitest tests for Market page (0% coverage)
description: - type: task

title: Mission and Leaderboard Improvements
description: Expand the DailyMission player selector beyond 12 players, fix Leaderboard trader column alignment, and rename misleading Weekly Gain header.

title: Add search/filter and expand player selector in DailyMission
description: The mission player selector only shows 12 players (players.slice(0, 12)). Add a search input to filter players by name/team, and show all matching players in a scrollable container. Audit issue: H-6.
design: Modify src/components/DailyMission/DailyMission.tsx. Add searchQuery state. Replace players.slice(0, 12) (line 245) with a filter on name/team. Add search input above selector chips. Add max-height: 300px; overflow-y: auto; to .selector-chips in DailyMission.module.css.
acceptance_criteria:
1. All available players are accessible in the selector (not limited to 12)
2. A search/filter input allows typing to filter by name or team
3. Player list is scrollable when showing many results
4. Existing pick functionality continues to work

title: Fix Leaderboard trader column alignment
description: The trader column uses inline span elements for avatar and name without explicit flex alignment, causing inconsistent vertical alignment. Add flex layout. Audit issue: M-6.
design: Modify src/pages/Leaderboard/Leaderboard.module.css. Add to .col-trader: display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px;
acceptance_criteria:
1. Avatar emoji and trader name are vertically centered and consistently aligned
2. Alignment is consistent across different browsers

title: Rename Weekly Gain column header to Gain
description: The leaderboard Weekly Gain column header implies time-based tracking that does not exist in this demo app. Rename to Gain to accurately describe the data. Audit issue: L-6.
design: Modify src/pages/Leaderboard/Leaderboard.tsx line 70. Change Weekly Gain to Gain.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Column header reads Gain instead of Weekly Gain
2. No other functional changes

title: Timeline and Scenario Improvements
description: Fix timeline time filters to use scenario-relative dates, add live simulation indicator for non-dev users, add scenario loading state, fix hardcoded LiveTicker text, map raw event type strings to display labels, and handle future-dated events.

title: Fix Timeline time filters to use scenario-relative dates
description: The Today and This Week time filters compare against new Date(), but scenario data uses hardcoded past timestamps. Change filters to be relative to the latest event timestamp in the current scenario data. Audit issue: H-4.
design: Modify src/pages/Timeline/Timeline.tsx lines 159-169. Replace const now = new Date() with const scenarioNow = new Date(allEvents[0]?.timestamp || Date.now()) since events are sorted newest-first. Use scenarioNow for Today and This Week comparisons.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Today filter shows events from the same day as the scenario latest event timestamp
2. This Week filter shows events from the 7 days leading up to the scenario latest timestamp
3. All Time filter continues to show all events
4. Filters work correctly across all demo scenarios

title: Add simplified live simulation indicator for non-dev users
description: The TimelineDebugger is hidden behind isDevMode(). Add a simplified indicator visible to all users in Live and Super Bowl scenarios showing the simulation is active with a play/pause toggle. Audit issue: H-7.
design: Modify src/components/TimelineDebugger/TimelineDebugger.tsx. Add a second rendering path: when !isDevMode() and scenario is live or superbowl, return a minimal div with play/pause button and Live simulation label. Use existing isPlaying/setIsPlaying from useSimulation(). Add new CSS classes in TimelineDebugger.module.css for the simplified view.
acceptance_criteria:
1. In Live or Super Bowl scenarios, a visible indicator shows the simulation is active
2. The indicator includes a play/pause toggle
3. Full TimelineDebugger with scrubbing/rewind remains dev-only
4. Indicator does not appear in non-live scenarios

title: Add loading state to ScenarioToggle during scenario switches
description: When switching scenarios, the tab highlights instantly but data may still be loading. Add a brief visual loading indicator to the active tab. Audit issue: M-9.
design: Modify src/components/ScenarioToggle/ScenarioToggle.tsx. Import scenarioLoading from useScenario(). Add conditional class to active tab: ${scenarioLoading ? styles['loading'] : \\}. Add .loading class to ScenarioToggle.module.css with subtle pulse animation.
acceptance_criteria:
1. During scenario loading, active tab shows a subtle loading indicator
2. Loading state clears when data finishes loading
3. Quick switches between scenarios do not cause visual glitches

title: Replace hardcoded LiveTicker fallback text
description: The LiveTicker fallback text is hardcoded to MNF: Chiefs vs Bills - Live updates as they happen. Replace with a generic or data-driven fallback. Audit issue: L-4.
design: Modify src/components/LiveTicker/LiveTicker.tsx line 62. Change the hardcoded string to Live game updates as they happen or pull matchup description from currentData via useScenario().
acceptance_criteria:
1. Fallback text does not reference specific team names
2. Fallback reads something like Live game updates as they happen

title: Map raw event type strings to display labels in Timeline
description: Timeline event badges display raw strings like league_trade instead of human-readable labels like Trade. The getEventTypeLabel function returns reason.type which includes underscores. Audit issue: L-7.
design: Modify src/pages/Timeline/Timeline.tsx function getEventTypeLabel (line 67-69). Add a mapping: const TYPE_DISPLAY_LABELS = { league_trade: 'Trade', game_event: 'Game Update', news: 'News' }. Return reason?.eventType?.toUpperCase() || TYPE_DISPLAY_LABELS[reason?.type] || 'Event'.
acceptance_criteria:
1. league_trade displays as Trade in timeline event badges
2. game_event displays as the specific event type or Game Update
3. All displayed labels match the type filter dropdown labels for consistency

title: Filter out future-dated events relative to scenario date
description: In Midweek scenario (Wed Dec 4), some price change entries are dated Dec 6, breaking demo immersion. Filter or label events that fall after the scenario narrative date. Audit issue: L-8.
design: Modify src/pages/PlayerDetail/PlayerDetail.tsx price timeline section. Filter chartData to exclude entries where timestamp > scenarioDate. Derive scenario date from latest non-future event timestamp. Depends on Bead 13 establishing the scenario now pattern. Do not modify src/data/*.json files.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Price changes in PlayerDetail timeline do not show events dated after the scenario current date
2. Or events are visually marked as upcoming if after scenario date

title: Fix all Cypress E2E tests -- migrate selectors from CSS classes to data-testid

title: Accessibility

acceptance_criteria:
1. eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y installed and configured in eslint.config.js
2. npm run lint passes with zero a11y warnings or errors
3. All img tags have alt attributes
4. All form inputs have associated labels or aria-label
5. No autofocus misuse flagged

title: Add ARIA attributes and focus indicators
description: Add ARIA roles, labels, and descriptions to all interactive elements. Ensure visible focus indicators on all focusable elements. Add aria-label to icon-only buttons (heart, close, trade buttons, etc.).
acceptance_criteria:
1. All icon-only buttons have aria-label attributes
2. All interactive elements have visible focus indicators (outline or equivalent)
3. Navigation has proper ARIA roles (nav, main, complementary)
4. Toast notifications have role=alert or aria-live=polite
5. Modal components have proper ARIA: role=dialog, aria-modal=true, aria-labelledby

title: Add keyboard navigation support
description: Ensure all interactive flows are navigable via keyboard only (Tab, Enter, Escape, arrow keys). Focus trap for modals. Skip navigation link. Tab through scenario toggle, player cards, trade forms.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Tab navigation works through all page flows without mouse
2. Modals trap focus and release on close (Onboarding, Glossary, PlayoffAnnouncement)
3. Escape key closes modals and popups
4. Skip-to-main-content link present
5. Player cards and trade buttons are keyboard-activatable (Enter/Space)
6. Scenario toggle tabs navigable with arrow keys

title: Add secondary indicators for color-coded values
description: Color-coded gain/loss indicators (red/green) currently have no alternative indicator for color-blind users. Add directional icons or text labels alongside color to convey the same information.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Gain/loss values show an up/down arrow icon in addition to green/red coloring
2. Price change indicators have aria-label describing direction and magnitude
3. Portfolio gain/loss cards have secondary text or icon indicator
4. Leaderboard and player card price changes have secondary indicator
5. Information is conveyed without relying solely on color

title: Research: Exploratory UI Audit via Browser Testing
description:
Start the dev server and manually walk through the entire McQueen app in the
browser (via cursor-ide-browser MCP). Simulate 4 user personas and document
every UI issue, UX friction point, and visual bug encountered.

## Personas to simulate

1. **New User** — Clear localStorage to trigger onboarding. Walk through the
   onboarding flow, browse the market for the first time, open a player detail,
   make a first buy, check portfolio.

2. **Active Trader** — Buy several players, sell some, check portfolio value
   and P&L, manage watchlist (add/remove stars), revisit player details to
   verify price changes after trades.

3. **Mission Player** — Open Daily Mission, pick 3 risers and 3 fallers,
   reveal results, check leaderboard standings.

4. **Scenario Explorer** — Switch between all 3 scenarios (Midweek, Live,
   Playoffs). In Live mode, use the Timeline Debugger (play, pause, scrub).
   Observe the Playoff announcement modal. Verify LiveTicker behavior.

## What to look for

- Visual bugs (overlapping elements, clipped text, broken layouts, misaligned
  components, missing images/icons)
- Interaction bugs (buttons that don't respond, broken navigation, stale state
  after actions)
- UX friction (confusing flows, missing feedback after actions, unclear
  affordances, dead ends)
- Data display issues (wrong formats, missing units, stale prices, chart
  rendering problems)
- Accessibility gaps (missing focus indicators, poor contrast, no keyboard nav)
- Console errors or warnings during normal usage
- Performance issues (slow transitions, janky animations, long loads)

## Process

1. Run `npm run dev` to start the Vite dev server
2. Use browser_navigate to open the app (default http://localhost:5173)
3. For each persona, use browser_snapshot before and after key interactions
4. Document every issue with: description, steps to reproduce, severity
   (Critical/High/Medium/Low), and a screenshot reference if possible
5. Check the browser console for errors/warnings after each major interaction
6. Produce output artifact at `.ralph/research/ui-audit.md`

## Pages to visit

Every page must be visited at least once across the 4 personas:

- Market (home) — browse, sort, filter
- PlayerDetail — view price history, ESPN content tiles, buy/sell
- Portfolio — holdings, P&L, total value
- Watchlist — starred players, add/remove
- Mission — daily picks, reveal, scoring
- Leaderboard — rankings, league data
- Timeline — debugger controls (Live scenario only)

## Output format

The output artifact (`.ralph/research/ui-audit.md`) must contain:

1. **Executive summary** — total issues found, breakdown by severity
2. **Issues by severity** — grouped as Critical, High, Medium, Low, each with:
   - Title
   - Affected page/component
   - Description of the problem
   - Steps to reproduce
   - Expected vs actual behavior
3. **Console errors** — any JS errors or warnings captured
4. **Recommended fix priority** — ordered list of what to fix first

## Acceptance criteria

1. All 4 personas fully walked through (onboarding, trading, mission,
   scenario switching)
2. Every page visited at least once (Market, PlayerDetail, Portfolio,
   Watchlist, Mission, Leaderboard, Timeline)
3. Output artifact exists at `.ralph/research/ui-audit.md` with:
   - Issues grouped by severity
   - Each issue has: title, description, repro steps, affected page/component
   - Summary stats (total issues by severity)
   - Recommended fix priority order
4. Console errors captured and included in findings

title: Testing Infrastructure
description: Bring unit test coverage from 3 tests to 50+. Add comprehensive unit tests for all services, contexts, and critical business logic. Verify existing Cypress E2E suite passes throughout.

title: Add unit tests for service modules
description: Add comprehensive unit tests for all service modules: sentimentEngine (keyword matching, negation, position-specific), espnService (cache, fallback, mocked fetch), priceCalculator (impact ranges, confidence weighting), priceResolver (override priority, history fallback, user impact), storageService (read/write, migration, corruption). Target >90% branch coverage per module.
acceptance_criteria:
1. services/__tests__/sentimentEngine.test.ts with tests for: keyword matching, negation detection, position-specific scoring, edge cases
2. services/__tests__/espnService.test.ts with tests for: cache hit/miss/expiry, proxy fallback, error handling (mocked fetch)
3. services/__tests__/priceResolver.test.ts with tests for: overrides priority, history fallback, user impact multiplier, missing player
4. services/__tests__/storageService.test.ts with tests for: read/write, version migration, corrupt data, missing keys
5. Each test file achieves >90% branch coverage for its module
6. All tests pass via npm run test:run

title: Add unit tests for simulation engines
description: Add comprehensive unit tests for both SimulationEngine implementations: TimelineSimulationEngine (timeline construction, tick advancement, stop at boundary, price update callbacks) and EspnSimulationEngine (article processing, deduplication, sentiment-to-price pipeline, refresh). Uses mocks for external dependencies.
acceptance_criteria:
1. services/__tests__/simulationEngine.test.ts with tests for both implementations
2. TimelineSimulationEngine tests: build timeline, advance tick, stop at end, emit price updates
3. EspnSimulationEngine tests: process articles, deduplicate, match players, calculate prices
4. External dependencies (fetch, espnService) are mocked
5. >90% branch coverage
6. All tests pass via npm run test:run

title: Add unit tests for context providers
description: Add unit tests for all four domain context providers using React Testing Library's renderHook. Test ScenarioContext (load, switch, version), SimulationContext (engine lifecycle, reset), TradingContext (buy/sell, accounting, reset), SocialContext (watchlist, mission, leaderboard).
acceptance_criteria:
1. context/__tests__/ScenarioContext.test.tsx: initial load, scenario switching, version increment
2. context/__tests__/SimulationContext.test.tsx: engine selection, play/pause, reset on version change
3. context/__tests__/TradingContext.test.tsx: buy/sell, cash accounting, scenario reset
4. context/__tests__/SocialContext.test.tsx: watchlist CRUD, mission scoring, leaderboard ranking
5. Each context achieves >90% branch coverage
6. Total unit test count across all suites exceeds 50

title: Verify Cypress E2E suite passes end-to-end
description: Run the full Cypress E2E suite against the refactored codebase. Fix any selector or import path issues caused by the refactor. All 75 existing tests should pass with minimal modifications.
acceptance_criteria:
1. npm run cy:run passes with 0 failures
2. All 75 existing E2E tests pass (or if any needed selector updates, changes are minimal and documented)
3. No behavioral regressions — all 8 user flows work correctly
4. vitest full suite runs in under 10 seconds

title: Research: Understand Current POC Behavior
description:
Audit the current mcqueen POC to document user flows, component boundaries, data flow through GameContext, service responsibilities, and known pain points. Produce a written artifact that the Spec issue can reference.

Key areas to cover:
- All 7 pages (Market, PlayerDetail, Portfolio, Watchlist, Mission, Leaderboard, Timeline) and their interactions
- The 3 demo scenarios (midweek, live, playoffs) and how they drive behavior
- GameContext.jsx as the single state management hub
- Services layer (espnService, priceCalculator, sentimentEngine)
- Data layer (static JSON scenario files in src/data/)
- Chrome extension integration
- Onboarding and first-trade flows
acceptance_criteria:
1. All user flows documented (Market browse, Player detail, Portfolio/Watchlist, Daily Mission, Leaderboard, Scenario switching, Onboarding)
2. Component and data-flow diagram produced (covering src/context/GameContext.jsx, src/services/, src/data/)
3. Must-keep behaviors explicitly listed (e.g., 3-scenario simulation, live-ticker, onboarding flow, chrome extension integration)
4. Known pain points and tech debt cataloged with severity

title: Verify coverage targets met
description: - type: task

title: Service Layer Refactor
description: Convert existing services to TypeScript and extract new pure modules (priceResolver, simulationEngine, storageService) from GameContext. Creates the business logic layer that the context split depends on.

title: Convert sentimentEngine to TypeScript
description: Rename sentimentEngine.js to sentimentEngine.ts and add type annotations. Define SentimentResult interface. No structural changes needed — the module is already well-isolated.
acceptance_criteria:
1. services/sentimentEngine.ts with full type annotations
2. SentimentResult type exported: { sentiment, magnitude, confidence, keywords, scores }
3. analyzeSentiment() has typed parameters and return type
4. No any types in the file
5. Existing priceCalculator.test.js still passes (it depends on sentimentEngine)

title: Convert priceCalculator to TypeScript
description: Rename priceCalculator.js to priceCalculator.ts and add type annotations. Define PriceImpact and PriceResult interfaces. Uses SentimentResult type from sentimentEngine.
acceptance_criteria:
1. services/priceCalculator.ts with full type annotations
2. PriceImpact and PriceResult types exported
3. All public functions have typed parameters and return types
4. Existing priceCalculator.test.js still passes (rename to .ts)
5. No any types

title: Convert espnService to TypeScript
description: Rename espnService.js to espnService.ts and add type annotations. Define Article, GameEvent, and NormalizedNews interfaces. Uses types from src/types/espn.ts.
acceptance_criteria:
1. services/espnService.ts with full type annotations
2. Uses Article and GameEvent types from types/espn.ts
3. fetchNFLNews, fetchTeamNews, fetchPlayerNews, fetchScoreboard all typed
4. Cache utility functions typed
5. No any types
6. Build succeeds

title: Extract priceResolver as pure module
description: Extract getEffectivePrice logic (GameContext lines 431-448) and helper functions (getCurrentPriceFromHistory, getChangePercentFromHistory, getMoveReasonFromHistory, etc. lines 58-134) into a new services/priceResolver.ts module. Pure functions with no React dependency, making them independently testable.
acceptance_criteria:
1. services/priceResolver.ts exports getEffectivePrice as a pure function taking (playerId, priceOverrides, userImpact, players)
2. Helper functions (getCurrentPriceFromHistory, getChangePercentFromHistory, getMoveReasonFromHistory, getLatestContentFromHistory, getAllContentFromHistory) exported
3. Zero React imports — purely computational
4. Unit tests with >90% branch coverage: overrides priority, history fallback, user impact multiplier, missing player
5. GameContext (or successor) imports from priceResolver instead of defining inline

title: Create SimulationEngine interface and TimelineSimulationEngine
description: Define a SimulationEngine interface that unifies tick-based and event-driven simulation modes (addresses H2). Implement TimelineSimulationEngine that drives live/superbowl scenarios by advancing through the unified timeline on a 3-second interval. Extracts logic from GameContext lines 111-134 (buildUnifiedTimeline) and lines 379-428 (tick useEffect).
acceptance_criteria:
1. SimulationEngine interface defined with start(), stop(), tick(), getPrice(), onPriceUpdate callback
2. TimelineSimulationEngine implements the interface for live/superbowl modes
3. buildUnifiedTimeline extracted as a pure function
4. Unit tests verify: timeline construction from player data, tick advancement, stop at end of timeline, price update callbacks
5. No React dependency — engine is a plain TypeScript class
6. Adding a third simulation mode would only require a new class implementing SimulationEngine

title: Create EspnSimulationEngine
description: Implement EspnSimulationEngine behind the SimulationEngine interface. Encapsulates ESPN API polling, article-to-player matching, sentiment analysis, and price calculation. Extracts logic from GameContext lines 213-313 (fetchAndProcessEspnNews) and lines 316-333 (auto-refresh effect).
acceptance_criteria:
1. EspnSimulationEngine implements SimulationEngine interface
2. Encapsulates: ESPN news fetching, article deduplication, player matching via searchTerms, sentiment analysis, price calculation
3. Uses espnService, sentimentEngine, and priceCalculator as injected dependencies (testable with mocks)
4. Unit tests with mocked fetch: article processing, deduplication, sentiment-to-price pipeline, refresh interval
5. No React dependency — engine is a plain TypeScript class
6. Emits price updates through the same onPriceUpdate callback as TimelineSimulationEngine

title: Tech Stack: Confirm Tooling and Validation Approach
description:
Lock down the validation and quality tooling that will guard the refactor loop. Current baseline: React 19, Vite 5.4, ESLint 9, no TypeScript, no tests, no E2E.

Decisions needed:
- TypeScript migration strategy (full migration vs. incremental .jsx -> .tsx)
- Test framework (Vitest is the natural fit given Vite)
- E2E tool (Playwright vs. Cypress) with rationale
- a11y validation approach (axe-core, eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y, or both)
- Whether to add Prettier or stick with ESLint formatting rules

Produce a list of concrete setup tasks that will be filed as child issues under the Issue Breakdown.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Decision recorded: TypeScript (full migration vs. incremental .jsx -> .tsx)
2. Test framework chosen and bootstrap task identified (Vitest likely, given Vite)
3. E2E tool chosen (Playwright vs. Cypress) with rationale
4. a11y validation approach decided (axe-core, eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y, or both)
5. List of setup tasks produced (e.g., add vitest config, add tsconfig.json, add playwright config)
6. All decisions documented with rationale

title: Add Prettier
description: Install prettier + eslint-config-prettier. Create .prettierrc with minimal config (printWidth, singleQuote, trailingComma). Add format and format:check scripts to package.json. Update eslint.config.js to include prettier compat. Run initial format pass on the codebase.

title: Clean up @types packages and add jsconfig.json
description: Keep @types/react and @types/react-dom for editor IntelliSense (JSDoc type imports). Add a jsconfig.json to formalize the JS-only setup with compilerOptions that enable IntelliSense without TypeScript compilation.

title: Extract magic numbers to src/constants.js
description: Create src/constants.js with named exports for magic numbers scattered across the codebase: TICK_INTERVAL_MS (3000), ESPN_REFRESH_MS (60000), USER_IMPACT_FACTOR (0.001), INITIAL_CASH (10000), and any others found. Update all references. Addresses pain point L2 from the POC audit.

title: Add Vitest tests for remaining 0% components (PlayoffModal, MiniLeaderboard, LiveTicker)
description: - type: task

title: Data Layer: Types, Storage, and Dynamic Loading
description: Define TypeScript type contracts for all data shapes, create a versioned storage service, and convert static JSON imports to dynamic loading. Establishes the typed foundation that services and contexts build on.

title: Create TypeScript type definitions
description: Create src/types/ directory with typed interfaces for all data shapes: ScenarioData, Player, PriceHistoryEntry, PriceReason, ContentItem, Portfolio, Holding, TimelineEntry, HistoryEntry, MissionPicks, LeaderboardEntry, Article, GameEvent, LeagueMember, LeagueHoldings. These types are the foundation for all TypeScript conversions.
acceptance_criteria:
1. src/types/scenario.ts defines ScenarioData, Player, PriceHistoryEntry, PriceReason, ContentItem
2. src/types/trading.ts defines Portfolio, Holding
3. src/types/simulation.ts defines TimelineEntry, HistoryEntry, SimulationMode
4. src/types/social.ts defines MissionPicks, MissionScore, LeaderboardEntry, LeagueHolding
5. src/types/espn.ts defines Article, GameEvent, SentimentResult
6. src/types/index.ts re-exports all types
7. Types match the actual data shapes in existing JSON files and GameContext

title: Convert constants and utils to TypeScript
description: Rename constants.js to constants.ts and all utils/*.js to utils/*.ts. Add type annotations. These are pure modules with no React dependency, making them the easiest conversion targets.
acceptance_criteria:
1. constants.ts exports typed constants (INITIAL_CASH: number, STORAGE_KEYS: Record, etc.)
2. utils/formatters.ts, utils/playerImages.ts, utils/espnUrls.ts, utils/devMode.ts all converted
3. Existing formatters.test.js still passes (rename to .ts if straightforward)
4. No any types — all exports have explicit type annotations
5. Build succeeds with no type errors

title: Create storageService with schema versioning
description: Extract localStorage logic from GameContext (lines 137-187) into a typed storageService module. Add schema versioning so that data shape changes don't silently corrupt saved state. Validates data on read and provides migration support.
acceptance_criteria:
1. src/services/storageService.ts exists with typed read/write functions
2. Storage entries include a schema version number
3. read() validates the stored data shape and returns a default on corruption
4. Migration function handles version upgrades (e.g., v1 -> v2 schema change)
5. Unit tests cover: happy path read/write, corrupt data recovery, version migration, missing key
6. No direct localStorage calls remain in GameContext (or successors) — all go through storageService

title: Convert scenario data loading to dynamic import()
description: Replace the static JSON imports at the top of GameContext (lines 10-15) with dynamic import() calls. Each scenario JSON becomes a separate Vite chunk loaded on demand. Only the active scenario is fetched. This addresses pain points H3 (1MB bundle) and H4 (direct JSON imports).
acceptance_criteria:
1. No static import statements for scenario JSON files in any source file
2. vite build produces separate chunks for each scenario JSON file
3. Scenario data is loaded asynchronously when the user selects a scenario
4. A loading state is shown while scenario data is being fetched
5. Initial bundle size reduced by ~400KB (verify with vite build output)
6. Cypress E2E tests still pass (scenarios load correctly)

title: State Management: Split GameContext
description: Replace the monolithic GameContext.jsx (957 lines) with four domain-specific contexts: ScenarioContext, SimulationContext, TradingContext, SocialContext. Each with its own provider, hook, and isolated re-render boundary.

title: Create ScenarioContext
description: Create ScenarioContext owning scenario selection, dynamic data loading, and player base data. Exposes scenario, currentData, players, and setScenario. Includes scenarioVersion counter for cross-context reset coordination. Uses storageService for persistence and dynamic import() for scenario data.
acceptance_criteria:
1. context/ScenarioContext.tsx with ScenarioProvider and useScenario hook
2. Scenario data loaded via dynamic import() (no static JSON imports)
3. scenarioVersion counter increments on every scenario switch
4. Loading state exposed while scenario data is being fetched
5. Scenario selection persisted via storageService
6. Unit tests cover: initial load, scenario switching, version increment, persistence

title: Create SimulationContext
description: Create SimulationContext owning simulation engine lifecycle, price overrides, unified timeline, tick state, and history. Instantiates the appropriate SimulationEngine implementation based on the active scenario. Resets on scenarioVersion change from ScenarioContext.
acceptance_criteria:
1. context/SimulationContext.tsx with SimulationProvider and useSimulation hook
2. Selects TimelineSimulationEngine for live/superbowl, EspnSimulationEngine for espn-live
3. Exposes: tick, isPlaying, priceOverrides, history, espnNews, espnLoading, espnError
4. Resets engine and state when scenarioVersion changes
5. history array capped at configurable max size (addresses L3)
6. goToHistoryPoint, applyPlayoffDilution, refreshEspnNews exposed as actions
7. Unit tests cover: engine selection, reset on scenario switch, history capping

title: Create TradingContext
description: Create TradingContext owning portfolio, cash, and user price impact. Exposes buyShares and sellShares actions. Uses priceResolver for effective price computation. Resets portfolio to startingPortfolio and cash to INITIAL_CASH on scenarioVersion change.
acceptance_criteria:
1. context/TradingContext.tsx with TradingProvider and useTrading hook
2. buyShares/sellShares use priceResolver.getEffectivePrice (not inline computation)
3. Portfolio and cash persisted via storageService
4. Resets on scenarioVersion change (portfolio to startingPortfolio, cash to INITIAL_CASH, userImpact cleared)
5. getPortfolioValue memoized (addresses M7)
6. Unit tests cover: buy/sell mechanics, insufficient cash, insufficient shares, scenario reset, persistence

title: Create SocialContext
description: Create SocialContext owning watchlist, mission, and leaderboard functionality. Watchlist persisted via storageService (persists across scenario switches). Mission state resets on scenarioVersion change. Leaderboard uses real league member data.
acceptance_criteria:
1. context/SocialContext.tsx with SocialProvider and useSocial hook
2. Watchlist ops: addToWatchlist, removeFromWatchlist, isWatching — persisted via storageService
3. Mission ops: setMissionPick, clearMissionPick, revealMission, resetMission, getMissionScore
4. getLeaderboardRankings uses real league data (fixes M2 — no hardcoded fake data)
5. getLeagueHoldings works correctly
6. Mission state resets on scenarioVersion change; watchlist persists
7. Unit tests cover: watchlist CRUD, mission scoring, leaderboard ranking, scenario reset

title: Wire provider composition and remove GameContext
description: Wire up the four new context providers in App.tsx in the correct nesting order (ScenarioProvider > SimulationProvider > TradingProvider > SocialProvider > ToastProvider > OnboardingProvider > BrowserRouter). Update all component imports from useGame() to the appropriate domain hooks (useScenario, useSimulation, useTrading, useSocial). Delete GameContext.jsx.
acceptance_criteria:
1. App.tsx uses the new provider composition order per spec section 1.1
2. All components updated: useGame() calls replaced with domain-specific hooks
3. GameContext.jsx deleted from the codebase
4. No component imports useGame — only useScenario, useSimulation, useTrading, useSocial
5. Changing watchlist does NOT re-render Market or PlayerDetail (verify S2)
6. All Cypress E2E tests pass

title: Layout and Header Repair
description: Fix layout issues: sidebar sticky positioning overlapping header, Total Value clipping in header, and content appearing below the fold on all pages.

title: Fix Market sidebar sticky positioning to account for header
description: The market sidebar has top: 16px for sticky positioning, but the sticky header is 64px tall. On scroll, the sidebar slides under the header. Update the top value to account for header height. Audit issue: M-4.
design: Modify src/pages/Market/Market.module.css line 134. Change top: 16px to top: calc(var(--header-height) + 16px). The --header-height CSS variable is defined globally in src/index.css.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Market sidebar sticks below the header on scroll, not behind it
2. Sidebar top value uses calc(var(--header-height) + 16px) or equivalent
3. Sidebar remains correctly positioned across different viewport sizes

title: Fix header Total Value clipping on scenarios with badges
description: The header Total Value label and dollar amount are clipped when scenario tabs include LIVE/ESPN badges. The header-left and header-right both have flex: 1 while header-center has flex: 2. When center content expands, it squeezes the right section. Audit issue: M-10.
design: Modify src/components/Layout/Layout.module.css. Change header-right to flex: 0 0 auto; min-width: fit-content; to prevent shrinking. Change header-center from flex: 2 to flex: 1 1 auto with min-width: 0; overflow: hidden; so center shrinks before right.
acceptance_criteria:
1. TOTAL VALUE label and dollar amount are fully visible on all scenarios including ESPN Live and Live Game
2. Header layout does not overflow on desktop viewports (>768px)
3. Mobile responsive layout still works correctly

title: Reduce header/nav vertical footprint to show content above the fold
description: The header (~64px) plus nav (~48px) push all page content below the initial viewport. Users see a mostly empty dark page and must scroll. Reduce vertical space consumed by header area. Audit issue: M-11.
design: Modify src/components/Layout/Layout.module.css. Reduce header height from 64px to ~52px (update --header-height). Reduce nav padding from 12px 24px to 8px 24px. Reduce main-content top padding from 24px to 16px. Test on all 6 nav pages to ensure nothing breaks.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Main page content is visible in the initial viewport without scrolling on standard desktop viewports (1080p+)
2. Header height is reduced or nav is more compact
3. All header elements remain accessible
4. Mobile layout still works

title: Add data-testid attributes to source components for Cypress selectors
description: - type: task

title: Accessibility Improvements
description: Add accessible alternative to PlayerCard sparkline, add keyboard dismissal to Toast notifications, and increase Watchlist remove button touch target.

title: Add accessible alternative to PlayerCard sparkline chart
description: The Recharts sparkline in PlayerCard renders as SVG with no accessible description. Screen reader users get no information about the price trend. Add an aria-label describing the trend. Audit issue: M-1.
design: Modify src/components/PlayerCard/PlayerCard.tsx. Wrap the ResponsiveContainer in a div with role="img" and aria-label={`7-day price trend: ${isUp ? 'up' : 'down'} ${Math.abs(player.changePercent).toFixed(1)}%`}.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Sparkline chart container has aria-label describing the trend (e.g. 7-day price trend: up 5.2%)
2. Chart has role=img to indicate it is a non-interactive image
3. Screen readers convey the price trend information

title: Add keyboard dismissal and ARIA improvements to Toast
description: Toast notifications cannot be dismissed via keyboard. The container has role=status and aria-live=polite, but no keyboard support exists. Add Escape key handling. Audit issue: M-7.
design: Modify src/components/Toast/Toast.tsx. Add onKeyDown handler to the toast container that listens for Escape and calls removeToast on the last toast. Ensure close button has proper focus. The close button already has aria-label="Dismiss notification".
acceptance_criteria:
1. Pressing Escape dismisses the most recent toast
2. Individual toast close buttons are focusable via Tab
3. role=status and aria-live=polite on the container are preserved
4. Toasts continue to auto-dismiss after the timeout

title: Increase Watchlist remove button touch target
description: The remove button on watchlist cards is a small positioned icon. Increase its touch target to meet WCAG 44x44px minimum. Audit issue: M-8.
design: Modify src/pages/Watchlist/Watchlist.module.css. Add to .remove-button: min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;
acceptance_criteria:
1. Remove button has a minimum tap area of 44x44px
2. Visual appearance can remain compact (hit area extends via padding)
3. Button remains correctly positioned on the card

title: Minor Polish
description: Cosmetic and edge-case fixes: chart tooltip truncation, EventMarkerPopup overflow, portfolio responsive columns, search input consistency, mock buyback holdings, and welcome banner border.

title: Fix PlayerDetail chart tooltip truncation and add date context
description: Price chart tooltip always truncates headlines to 40 chars with unconditional ellipsis and hides the date label. Fix truncation to be conditional and show the date. Audit issue: M-5.
design: Modify src/pages/PlayerDetail/PlayerDetail.tsx lines 369-379. For the formatter: conditionally truncate at word boundary. Remove labelStyle={{ display: 'none' }} and format the label to show date from entry.timestamp.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Tooltip shows full headline when <=40 characters (no trailing ellipsis)
2. Tooltip truncates at word boundaries when headline >40 characters
3. Tooltip shows the date/time of the hovered price point

title: Add viewport boundary checking to EventMarkerPopup
description: The EventMarkerPopup position is set as { x: cx, y: cy + 20 } with no boundary checking. Near edges, the popup may overflow. Audit issue: L-1.
design: Modify src/pages/PlayerDetail/PlayerDetail.tsx in handleEventClick (line 162). After computing position, check against chartContainerRef bounds. Adjust position if popup would overflow.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Popup repositions to stay within chart container bounds
2. Near right edge, popup shifts left
3. Near bottom edge, popup shifts above the marker

title: Make Portfolio responsive column hiding class-based instead of nth-child
description: Portfolio page uses nth-child selectors for responsive column hiding, which is fragile. Switch to class-based hiding. Audit issue: L-2.
design: Modify src/pages/Portfolio/Portfolio.tsx to add class names to each column span. Modify src/pages/Portfolio/Portfolio.module.css to replace nth-child selectors with class-based media queries.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Responsive column hiding uses class names instead of nth-child
2. At 900px breakpoint, Avg Cost and Value columns are hidden
3. At 600px breakpoint, Shares column is additionally hidden
4. Header and row columns remain aligned at all breakpoints

title: Standardize search input type across pages
description: Market uses type=search while Timeline uses type=text for search inputs. Standardize to type=search. Audit issue: L-3.
design: Modify src/pages/Timeline/Timeline.tsx line 299. Change type="text" to type="search".
acceptance_criteria:
1. Both Market and Timeline search inputs use type=search
2. Search functionality is unchanged

title: Replace mock buyback holdings with actual portfolio data
description: PlayoffAnnouncementModal uses MOCK_BUYBACK_HOLDINGS instead of actual portfolio from TradingContext. Replace with real portfolio data. Audit issue: L-5.
design: Modify src/components/PlayoffAnnouncementModal/PlayoffAnnouncementModal.tsx. Import useTrading and portfolio from TradingContext. Replace MOCK_BUYBACK_HOLDINGS references with portfolio. Filter to only show holdings for buybackPlayers. Remove the MOCK_BUYBACK_HOLDINGS constant.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Buyback modal shows actual user holdings for eliminated-team players
2. Buyback proceeds calculated from actual shares and prices
3. If user has no eliminated-team holdings, show No holdings to buy back

title: Replace welcome banner debug-style border with subtle styling
description: The welcome banner has border: 1px solid var(--color-primary) which renders as a prominent red border resembling a debug artifact. Replace with subtle border. Audit issue: L-9.
design: Modify src/pages/Market/Market.module.css line 163. Change border: 1px solid var(--color-primary) to border: 1px solid var(--color-border). Optionally add border-left: 3px solid var(--color-primary) for visual accent.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Welcome banner border uses var(--color-border) or similarly subtle color
2. Banner blends with the app design system

title: Create shared test helpers (renderWithProviders + mockData)
description: - type: task
